description: Unique identifier assigned to each participant.
Unique identifier for a single trial within a session or condition.
Experimental block of 250 trials (block_1 to block_8 across two sessions) seperated by 5-min breaks.
Binary indicator of whether the participant's response was correct.
Stimulus type: mot = word, nonmot = nonword
The linguistic or visual stimulus presented to the participant in a given trial.
Participant's response (Is the stimulus a word?): oui = yes, non = no
Time taken by the participant to respond, in milliseconds.
Flag RT outliers like in the paper: RT < 200 ms, RT > 2000 ms, or rt-zscore > 3.
Unique identifier for each stimulus item.
Experimental session (session_1 or session_2). Sessions were run on different days separated by no more than 1 week.
Z-scored RT within each participant. Minimizes influence of individual differences in processing speed.
